Suppose that you are in charge of taking ice cream orders for a class of 100
students. If each student orders exactly one flavor from Vanilla, Strawberry,
Chocolate and Pecan, how many different combinations of flavors are possible
for the 100 orders you are taking? Here are some examples of possible
combinations. You do not distinguish between individual students.

\begin{enumerate}[(a)]
\item $V = 30, S = 20, C = 40, P = 10$.
\item $V = 80, S = 0, C = 20, P = 0$.
\item $V = 0, S = 0, C = 0, P = 100$.
\end{enumerate}
